items.headline: U.S. Stocks Rally as Oil Concerns Ease, AI Trade Regains Ground
items.summary: U.S. stock markets closed higher on Tuesday, rebounding from earlier lows. All three major indexes ended in positive territory, driven by a decline in crude oil prices that eased inflationary concerns and a resurgence in AI-related stocks. However, geopolitical conflicts in the Middle East and rising oil prices continued to contribute to mixed global market sentiment, with U.S. futures slipping early in the day.

items.headline: Sable Offshore Corp. ($SOC)
items.summary: Sable Offshore Corp. led the monthly gains for March 2026, reflecting strong investor interest in the energy sector.

items.headline: Amprius Technologies, Inc. ($AMPX)
items.summary: Amprius Technologies showed significant upward momentum, indicating positive developments or market sentiment towards industrial innovations.

items.headline: ZipRecruiter, Inc. ($ZIP)
items.summary: ZipRecruiter experienced a notable surge, possibly driven by positive news in the communication services sector or a favorable outlook on the job market.

items.headline: U.S. Futures Slip Amid Rising Oil Prices
items.summary: While major indexes closed higher, U.S. stock futures for the S&P 500, Nasdaq 100, and Dow Jones Industrial Average showed declines in pre-market trading, reflecting renewed concerns over oil price spikes due to ongoing U.S.-Iran tensions.

items.headline: Sector Performance: Utilities and Technology Lead, Materials Lag
items.summary: The Utilities Select Sector SPDR (XLU) rose 1%, while the Materials Select Sector SPDR (XLB) fell 1%. Technology stocks, especially those related to AI, led the broader rally following advancements announced at Nvidia's GTC 2026 keynote. Other leading sectors in monthly rankings include Healthcare, Industrials, and Consumer Defensive.

items.headline: FOMC Meeting Underway Amid Inflationary Pressures and Geopolitical Conflict
items.summary: The Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meets today, March 17-18, 2026, with a high probability of holding interest rates steady in the 3.50% to 3.75% range. This decision is influenced by the economic impact of the Iran conflict and surging oil prices. Updated economic projections and the 'dot plot' are expected tomorrow, March 18.

items.headline: Mixed Economic Data Released for February
items.summary: February saw industrial production rise by 0.2%, with manufacturing output up 0.2% and mining growing 0.8%. However, utilities output fell 0.6%, and the NY Empire State Index for manufacturing unexpectedly declined to -0.2. Capacity utilization edged up to 76.3%.

items.headline: Key Companies Report Earnings Today
items.summary: Lululemon Athletica ($LULU), DocuSign ($DOCU), and Oklo ($OKLO) are among the notable companies scheduled to report earnings today. Steel Dynamics, Inc. ($STLD) also provided its first quarter 2026 earnings guidance, expecting meaningfully higher profitability from steel operations.
